close_reason: Read report-23, validated all 12 findings against live codebase, seeded bead-sse1 (SSE event stream) and bead-inv1 (input validation), appended triage to docs/research/report-23.md

title: Data layer: add real migration apply step to CI/CD pipeline
description: From report-21 (bead-6i3j). Currently migrate:ci = migrate:check + migrate:dry-run:expand only; the CD workflow also uses dry-run only. No real Postgres service is ever started and migrations are never applied against a live DB in CI/CD. This creates schema drift risk between code and deployed DB. Scope: (1) Add a postgres service container to the ci.yml GitHub Actions workflow (postgres:16-alpine, same as docker-compose). (2) Add a migrate:apply:ci script that applies expand migrations against the test DB. (3) Run store adapter integration tests against that live DB (replaces InMemorySqlClient for critical paths). (4) Gate the apply step on successful dry-run preflight. (5) Update CD workflow to run a migrate:apply job (not just dry-run) before deploying. Acceptance criteria: CI applies real migrations to Postgres service; integration tests run against real DB; CD applies migrations before code deployment.

title: SBOM generation + artefact signing + provenance attestations
description:
Concrete supply-chain integrity deliverables that make SLSA mapping non-theoretical. Without these, "supply-chain guardrails" remains aspirational.

Deliverables:
- SBOM generation: produce CycloneDX or SPDX SBOM for each release artefact (control plane image, worker image, Helm chart)
- Container image signing: sign OCI images using cosign or similar
- Provenance attestations: generate and publish SLSA-style provenance for each build (who built, what source, what build system, what dependencies)
- Verification documentation: how consumers verify signatures and provenance
- CI integration: SBOM + signing + attestation steps added to release pipeline

This is what turns "we care about supply chain" into verifiable evidence.

title: Release unit definition — Helm charts, image build/push, promotion flows
description:
Define the explicit release unit that turns code into something deployable and testable under production-like constraints.

Deliverables:
- Helm charts for control plane and worker (parameterised for env-specific config)
- Image build + push pipeline (multi-arch, tagged with git SHA + semver)
- Promotion flow: dev → staging → production (with gates)
- Chart versioning aligned with API versioning strategy
- Release checklist / runbook

This is not just "infra" — it is the bridge between "code works locally" and "code is deployable."

title: Architecture: add caching layer for hot policy/config reads (Redis or in-process TTL cache)
description: From report-23 bead-4m9a. Blocked by bead-0js5 (validate task). The control plane has no caching — every request to evaluate a policy or load workspace config hits Postgres and OpenFGA. Under load this will be a bottleneck. Scope: (1) Identify the 3-5 hottest read paths: workspace config, OpenFGA policy check results, pack definitions, workspace role lookups. (2) Design a CachePort interface: get(key): Promise<T | null>; set(key, value, ttlSeconds): Promise<void>; invalidate(key): Promise<void>; invalidatePattern(pattern): Promise<void>. (3) Implement RedisCacheAdapter and InMemoryTTLCacheAdapter (for dev/test). (4) Wrap the hot read paths with cache-aside pattern: check cache first; on miss, fetch from source and populate cache with appropriate TTL. (5) Add cache invalidation on write: when workspace config changes, invalidate the relevant cache keys. (6) Add cache hit/miss metrics to OTel (cache.hits, cache.misses counters). (7) Document TTL choices in a cache-strategy section in an ADR. Acceptance criteria: hot reads served from cache on repeat calls; cache invalidation on mutation; metrics instrumented; unit tests for both cache adapters; integration test confirming cache invalidation on write.

title: Data layer: add missing indexes, FK constraints, pagination, and remove SELECT *
description:
From report-21 (bead-6i3j). Blocked by bead-8k55 (validate task).

The domain_documents table and associated tables lack: FK constraints on cross-table references, indexes on query-path columns, and list queries lack LIMIT/OFFSET pagination.

Scope: (1) FK constraints — audit all migrations; add FK with appropriate ON DELETE behaviour for every cross-table reference. (2) Indexes — B-tree on all WHERE-clause columns; composite indexes for multi-column hot-path filters; partial indexes where appropriate. (3) Pagination — every list query method accepts LIMIT and OFFSET; HTTP endpoints expose page/pageSize; total count returned for UI pagination controls. (4) JSON schema validation — add CHECK constraint or application-layer schema validation on domain_documents JSON column before insert/update. (5) Remove SELECT * — replace with explicit column lists in all queries to prevent surprise breakage from schema changes.

Acceptance criteria: all cross-table refs have FK constraints; all WHERE columns have indexes; all list queries paginate; no SELECT * anywhere; CI data layer tests (parallel bead) confirm all above.

title: Read report-29: extract security findings, triage by severity, and seed hardening beads
description:
**Parent epic**: bead-y0ac (Security)
**Report**: docs/research/report-29.md

## What to do

Read the full 4-section report. For each security finding, assess current codebase state and assign CVSS-lite severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low). Any Critical or High finding must have a bead created immediately and blocked on nothing — they go to the top of the queue.

## Sections to work through

1. **Identity and Access Management**
   - Dev-mode static bearer token: find every code path that accepts a static token; confirm whether it's env-gated; note what happens if NODE_ENV=production is not set
   - JWT validation: find the JWT middleware; verify 'alg: none' is rejected; verify audience + issuer claims are validated; verify expiry is checked
   - OpenFGA integration: find where OpenFGA checks are applied; map which endpoints have policy checks and which rely only on workspace-role RBAC; note any gaps
   - Session management: check for token refresh logic; check for logout/revocation support

2. **Secrets and Cryptography**
   - Vault credential store: find the VaultCredentialStore adapter; verify lease handling, rotation, and fallback behaviour
   - SHA-256 evidence hashing: find the hash chain implementation; verify it's used consistently; check for any MD5 or SHA-1 usage anywhere in the codebase
   - Random ID generation: grep for Math.random() — any use in security-relevant ID generation is a Critical finding
   - Secret logging: grep for console.log, logger.info, etc. near secret/token/password/key variables; check error message formatting

3. **Network and Deployment**
   - HTTPS enforcement: find the HTTP server setup; check for redirect middleware or HSTS headers; check Helm/K8s ingress TLS config
   - CORS: find CORS config; check for wildcard origin; check whether it differs between dev and prod
   - Rate limiting: confirm rate-limiter is in-memory; note multi-replica risk; assess whether Redis store is already available in the stack
   - K8s secrets: scan Helm templates and K8s manifests for hardcoded secrets or base64-encoded values

4. **Development and QA**
   - Security scanning: verify Snyk/npm audit covers all workspaces; check for known CVEs in current lockfile
   - Auth rejection tests: grep for 401/403 in test files; note any endpoint that lacks an auth rejection test
   - OWASP Top-10 checklist: work through each item for the control plane API

## Output

Append  to docs/research/report-29.md with a severity-triage table. Create one bead per finding group. Any Critical finding gets priority=0 and no blockers.
close_reason: Report-29 triaged: 1 critical + 4 high fixed; seeded beads bead-pj5a, bead-8qmt, bead-rv3k, bead-7zzq for remaining gaps

title: Implement triage mode (rapid swipe-based approval UX)
description:
Build the "Tinder-style" triage mode for rapid classification and routing of approval requests. This is the high-throughput mode for processing approval queues quickly.

Requirements:
- Card-stack or swipeable interface showing approval summary cards
- Swipe/tap actions: Approve / Request Changes / Deny / Skip / Escalate
- Each card shows the approval shell summary (compact form)
- Contextual friction for high-risk approvals: swipe-to-approve disabled when risk is high or policy requires specialist — forces opening detail view
- Keyboard shortcuts for power users (a=approve, d=deny, r=request changes, s=skip)
- Queue management: filter by type, risk level, SLA urgency, assigned-to-me vs team
- Batch operations for homogeneous low-risk approvals (e.g., "approve all 12 staging deploys")

Safety guardrails:
- High-risk items require opening the detail view before deciding
- Policy-driven friction: require typed acknowledgement for certain categories
- Show prior decisions from other approvers
- Rationale required for deny/request-changes
- Visual risk indicators (color-coded risk bands)

title: Validate report-27 testing gaps: run coverage baseline and confirm untested modules
description:
Parent epic: bead-cyut. Blocked by: bead-4jpd (read task). Report: docs/research/report-27.md

Run actual coverage to confirm current state. The report may be out of date.

## Steps

Run coverage baseline from root and from apps/cockpit. Record: overall branch %, function %, line %.

For each called-out module, confirm: Does a .test.ts file exist? Does it have meaningful assertions? Approx branch coverage?

Modules to check:
- src/domain/ pack resolver
- src/domain/ SOD constraint evaluator
- src/application/ workflow orchestration use-cases
- src/infrastructure/ workspace-scoped rate-limiter
- src/infrastructure/ CloudEvent publishers
- Temporal worker (execution plane)

For each untested module: can it be instantiated with mock deps, or does it import singletons directly? If singletons: note the DI refactor needed before the module is testable.

CI config check:
- vitest.config.ts — coverage thresholds set?
- .github/workflows — coverage step or artifact upload?

Output: Record coverage baseline in docs/research/report-27.md session notes. Update each test-suite implementation bead with confirmed scope.
close_reason: Coverage baseline confirmed via bead-4jpd triage: all 6 modules have tests; thresholds configured (statements 83%, functions 88%, lines 85%); coverage artifact in CI. See docs/research/report-27.md triage section.

title: Security: require JWT issuer + audience in production bootstrap
description:
From report-29 (bead-nn3y). Currently PORTARIUM_JWT_ISSUER and PORTARIUM_JWT_AUDIENCE are optional. In production, an unset issuer/audience allows tokens from any issuer/for any audience to be accepted.

Scope:
(1) In tryBuildJoseAuthentication(), if PORTARIUM_JWKS_URI is set but neither PORTARIUM_JWT_ISSUER nor PORTARIUM_JWT_AUDIENCE is set, log a warning and continue (backwards-compatible).
(2) Add an explicit startup warning that without iss/aud validation, any token from the JWKS provider will be accepted for any service.
(3) Add a CI test that confirms the warning is emitted when only PORTARIUM_JWKS_URI is configured.
(4) Document the recommendation to always set PORTARIUM_JWT_ISSUER and PORTARIUM_JWT_AUDIENCE in the local-dev and production env docs.

Acceptance criteria: startup emits a visible warning when PORTARIUM_JWT_ISSUER or PORTARIUM_JWT_AUDIENCE is absent; unit tests cover the warning path; existing JWT tests still pass.
close_reason: Added getJoseAuthConfigWarnings() exported function to bootstrap; warns to stderr when PORTARIUM_JWT_ISSUER or PORTARIUM_JWT_AUDIENCE absent; 8 unit tests added.

title: Data layer: make Postgres pool sizing and timeout configuration explicit
description: From report-21 (bead-6i3j). NodePostgresSqlClient (src/infrastructure/postgresql/node-postgres-sql-client.ts) instantiates pg.Pool with only a connection string, relying on pg defaults (max=10, idleTimeoutMillis=10000, connectionTimeoutMillis=0). These defaults are not appropriate for all production workloads and the zero connectionTimeoutMillis can cause indefinite hangs under pool exhaustion. Scope: (1) Add explicit pool config parameters to NodePostgresSqlClientConfig: maxConnections, idleTimeoutMs, connectionTimeoutMs, statementTimeoutMs. (2) Map these from env vars: PORTARIUM_DB_POOL_MAX, PORTARIUM_DB_POOL_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS, PORTARIUM_DB_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT_MS, PORTARIUM_DB_STATEMENT_TIMEOUT_MS. (3) Set sensible non-zero defaults (e.g., connectionTimeoutMs=5000, statementTimeoutMs=30000). (4) Add tests asserting config is passed through to pool. Acceptance criteria: pool config is explicit and env-configurable; zero connectionTimeoutMs is not the default; tests confirm config propagation.
close_reason: Added explicit pool config to NodePostgresSqlClientConfig (maxConnections, idleTimeoutMs, connectionTimeoutMs=5000, statementTimeoutMs=30000). Added nodePostgresSqlClientConfigFromEnv() to read from env vars. 3 new tests. 2957 tests pass.

title: Docker Compose profiles — baseline + runtime/auth/cockpit/tools profiles
description: Restructure docker-compose.yml into stable baseline (Postgres, Temporal, MinIO, Vault, OTel+Grafana, always-on) plus optional profiles: runtime (control plane + worker), auth (Keycloak/OpenFGA), cockpit (UI dev server), tools (extra debugging/admin). Docker Compose profiles are the intended mechanism for single-file multi-use-case service management. The current Compose explicitly omits API+worker; profiles solve this without breaking the baseline.
close_reason: Already closed in git history (Docker Compose profiles)

title: kind-based deployment smoke for infra parity
description: Optional extension for later. kind runs local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container nodes — useful for verifying infra/kubernetes manifests locally before pushing to real clusters. Deliverables: kind cluster config, script to deploy control plane + worker via Helm charts into kind, smoke test that exercises health endpoints and basic API calls in a real K8s environment. Lower priority than Compose-based DX but valuable for infra parity testing.

title: Robotics semantic mapping + safety invariants for VDA 5050 / MassRobotics
description: VDA 5050 defines AGV-to-master-control interface. MassRobotics AMR standard enables cross-manufacturer state sharing but is explicitly NOT a safety system. Missing: what do you trust from these feeds, what is advisory-only, what evidence is required before issuing physical commands? Deliverables: semantic mapping to Portarium domain, trust classification (trusted/advisory/untrusted), safety invariants before physical commands, evidence requirements, validation that rejects commands violating invariants.

title: Security: document JWT short-expiry guidance and revocation risk (ADR)
description:
From report-29 (bead-nn3y). JWTs are stateless and cannot be revoked until expiry. This is a documented risk.

Scope:
(1) Add an ADR (docs/adr/ADR-XXXX-jwt-revocation-strategy.md) documenting:
  - Current state: stateless JWTs, no revocation
  - Risk: stolen tokens valid until exp claim
  - Recommended mitigations: short-lived tokens (15–60 min), refresh token flow, optional denylist for high-value operations
  - Decision: recommend short exp + documented denylist option for future implementation
(2) Update docs/getting-started/local-dev.md to note recommended exp values for dev vs prod tokens.

Acceptance criteria: ADR created and merged; local-dev doc updated.

title: Supported versions + patch cadence policy
description:
Define which versions receive security patches, how long they are supported, and what the expected patch cadence is. Ties directly into vulnerability handling and the vulnerability gate objective.

Deliverables:
- Supported versions table (in SECURITY.md and/or docs)
- Patch cadence commitment (e.g., critical CVEs within 48h, high within 1 week)
- End-of-life policy for old versions
- How fixes are disclosed (changelog, advisory, notification)

Matters for both users and contributors. Required for meaningful OpenSSF badge work.

title: Seed command — load canonical bundle into stores + optional evidence payloads
description: Convert existing canonical seed factories (src/domain/testing/canonical-seeds-v1.ts) into a runnable developer experience. Script that: selects a seed bundle, writes it into persistence stores (DB, MinIO), optionally provisions related state (policy/workflow entries, example approvals, evidence payloads). Exposed as npm run dev:seed. This is the bridge between strong domain test factories and a working local demo.

title: OCI images for API server + worker wired into Compose
description: Dockerfiles exist (infra/docker/control-plane.Dockerfile, infra/docker/worker.Dockerfile) but Compose does not run them by default. Wire images into Compose runtime profile, ensure env var parity with documented runtime-and-env.md surface, verify health endpoints work in containerised mode. This closes the gap between Dockerfiles exist and product is running in containers.

title: Data layer: fix N+1 queries and move in-memory filtering to SQL
description: From report-21 (bead-6i3j). Two related issues: (1) N+1: postgres-workforce-store-adapters.ts:106 calls listWorkforceMembersByIds using Promise.all over getById calls — one query per member. Fix by batching via WHERE document_id = ANY($1) and returning a map keyed by id. (2) In-memory filter: Several store adapter list methods fetch more rows than needed and filter/sort in Node.js. Move filtering, sorting, and pagination into SQL (aligned with query-builder.ts capabilities). Scope: (a) Fix workforce N+1 with batched ANY query. (b) Audit all store adapter list methods; replace fetch+filter patterns with SQL WHERE predicates and ORDER BY. (c) Ensure all list endpoints have LIMIT applied at SQL level (no unbounded full scans). (d) Add benchmarks / tests asserting query count. Acceptance criteria: no N+1 in workforce listing; all list queries have SQL-level limits; unit tests verify query patterns.
close_reason: Added listByIds to PostgresJsonDocumentStore using ANY($3::text[]) batch query. Updated InMemorySqlClient with selectByIds. Fixed listWorkforceMembersByIds N+1 pattern. 2957 tests pass.

title: Architecture: implement workspace-scoped SSE event stream endpoint
description: From report-23 (bead-4m9a) SSE finding. The location-events:stream route exists but a workspace-wide /events/stream endpoint is absent. Clients (Cockpit, SDK) need a durable event stream for run-started, approval-required, run-completed, and heartbeat events scoped to a workspace. Scope: (1) Add GET /v1/workspaces/{id}/events:stream route (SSE, text/event-stream). (2) Define EventStreamPort in application layer for publishing workspace events. (3) Implement in-memory broadcast adapter (for dev/test) and Redis Pub/Sub adapter (for production). (4) Emit events from existing use-cases: RunStarted, ApprovalRequired, ApprovalDecided, RunCompleted. (5) Add SSE keep-alive ping every 30s to prevent proxy timeout. (6) Add unit + integration tests. Acceptance criteria: SSE endpoint streams structured events; client can subscribe and receive real-time run/approval updates; tests pass.

title: Validate report-24 Cockpit UI/UX findings against live codebase
description:
**Parent epic**: bead-vox6 | **Blocked by**: bead-50j5 (read task)
**Report**: docs/research/report-24.md

## Validation checklist

### TriageCard monolith
- [ ] Find TriageCard component file; count actual lines; confirm it's still 750+ lines
- [ ] Map the sub-responsibilities (rendering, data fetching, state management, event handlers) — these become decomposition targets
- [ ] Find any existing sub-components extracted from it

### Hardcoded demo data
- [ ] Grep apps/cockpit/src/ for hardcoded IDs, names, email addresses, or demo-specific strings
- [ ] Find any import of fixture/seed data in production component files
- [ ] Check for any environment-conditional rendering of demo content

### Accessibility
- [ ] Run axe-core or Lighthouse accessibility audit on the built app (note baseline score)
- [ ] Find every icon-only button; check for aria-label
- [ ] Find status badge components; confirm they use more than colour to convey state
- [ ] Find modal/dialog components; check for focus trap and Escape key handling
- [ ] Find page-level layout; confirm skip-navigation link exists

### Error Boundaries
- [ ] Grep for ErrorBoundary in apps/cockpit/src/; confirm presence/absence
- [ ] Find async data-fetching components; check for loading state and error state handling

### Loading States
- [ ] Find every mutation handler (form submit, button click that calls API); confirm loading indicator
- [ ] Find every page that fetches data on mount; confirm skeleton or spinner

### Code Splitting
- [ ] Check Vite config for manual chunks or lazy() usage
- [ ] Check bundle analysis output if it exists

## Output

Update each implementation bead with confirmed file:line. Note axe-core baseline score in docs/research/report-24.md session notes.

title: Security: remove or hard-gate dev-mode static bearer token; harden JWT validation
description:
From report-29 (bead-y0ac). Blocked by bead-16hd (validate task). This is a P0 security hardening task.

The control plane has a dev-mode static bearer token fallback. This must never be reachable in non-local environments. JWT validation may also have gaps (alg:none, audience/issuer not validated).

Scope:

(1) Static bearer token — find every code path that accepts a static token; add a hard env-gate (must be explicitly enabled via ENABLE_DEV_AUTH=true, not just the absence of NODE_ENV=production); add startup assertion that fails the process if ENABLE_DEV_AUTH is set and NODE_ENV is not 'development' or 'test'; add a CI test that confirms the API returns 401 with a valid-looking token when dev auth is disabled.

(2) JWT validation hardening — find the JWT verify call; ensure algorithms array explicitly lists only allowed algorithms and never includes 'none'; validate audience claim against AUTH_AUDIENCE config; validate issuer claim against AUTH_ISSUER config; ensure nbf and exp are enforced (most JWT libraries do this by default but verify explicitly).

(3) Token revocation — check if logout/revocation is supported; if tokens are long-lived JWTs with no revocation, document the risk and consider short expiry + refresh token pattern.

(4) Auth rejection test suite — add tests for: missing Authorization header returns 401; malformed token returns 401; expired token returns 401; wrong audience returns 401; wrong issuer returns 401; valid token for wrong workspace returns 403.

Acceptance criteria: static token path requires explicit ENABLE_DEV_AUTH=true; process fails to start if ENABLE_DEV_AUTH and NODE_ENV != development/test; all JWT validation properties verified in unit tests; auth rejection test suite passes in CI.
close_reason: ENABLE_DEV_AUTH env gate + fatal startup assert + JWT audience/issuer/alg:none tests implemented

title: Data layer: add unit-of-work / withTransaction abstraction to SqlClient
description: From report-21 (bead-6i3j). SqlClient (src/infrastructure/postgresql/sql-client.ts) exposes only a query() method — no transaction boundary abstraction. This prevents atomically combining: (1) state writes to domain_documents or relational projections with (2) outbox row inserts, which is required for Transactional Outbox correctness. Without it, partial failures leave state written but event not enqueued, or vice versa. Scope: (a) Add withTransaction(fn: (txClient: SqlClient) => Promise<T>): Promise<T> to the SqlClient interface and NodePostgresSqlClient implementation (using pg.PoolClient + BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK). (b) Create TxSqlClient wrapper that ensures all queries in fn run within one connection/transaction. (c) Enforce in multi-write use-cases (run start + outbox, approval + outbox). (d) Add unit tests for transaction rollback on error and commit on success. (e) Update postgres-eventing.ts outbox enqueue to accept a tx client. Acceptance criteria: multi-write operations are atomic; failure in any step rolls back the entire transaction; tests prove rollback behaviour.
close_reason: Implemented withTransaction on SqlClient interface + NodePostgresSqlClient (BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK, nested tx reuses connection, pool injectable). Updated InMemorySqlClient + 4 test doubles. 6 new unit tests. 2937 tests pass.

title: LLM security controls — output validation, prompt-injection resistance, agency boundaries
description: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications: prompt injection, insecure output handling, sensitive info disclosure, excessive agency, overreliance. Deliverables: output validation/sanitisation before cockpit rendering, prompt-injection resistance in RAG flows, hard agency boundaries (AI advisor recommend-only, never auto-approve), cross-workspace data leak prevention in AI responses, overreliance guardrails (low-confidence indicators), adversarial test suite.

title: Cockpit: add error boundaries, loading states, and empty states across all async views
description: From report-24 bead-vox6. Blocked by bead-sy6l (validate task). Cockpit has no React error boundaries, no loading indicators on async actions, and errors silently fail. This violates Nielsen heuristic 1 (visibility of system status) and heuristic 9 (help users recover from errors). Scope: (1) Add a top-level ErrorBoundary wrapping the entire app; add per-section ErrorBoundaries so a broken widget doesn't crash the whole page; each boundary shows a user-friendly error card with a retry button and error code. (2) Identify every mutation (form submit, approval button, reject button, any action that calls the API); add loading state to the trigger (disabled + spinner) and success/failure feedback (toast or inline). (3) Identify every page/section that fetches data on mount; add skeleton loading component during fetch; add empty-state component when data returns empty; add error-state component when fetch fails with retry option. (4) Remove any silent error swallowing (catch blocks that do nothing). Acceptance criteria: no unhandled errors crash the page silently; every async action has loading + success/error feedback; every data-fetching view has skeleton + empty + error states; error boundaries in CI component tests.

title: CI-to-cloud OIDC trust policy hardening
description:
The infra plan includes OIDC federation for CI (GitHub Actions → cloud). Mis-scoped trust policies and missing claim constraints are frequent sources of pipeline fragility or over-privilege.

Deliverables:
- OIDC trust policy review: verify claim constraints (repo, branch, environment, workflow)
- Principle of least privilege for CI identity
- Document the trust boundary: what can a compromised CI workflow access?
- Test: verify workflow from a different branch/repo/org is rejected
- Audit trail: log OIDC token exchanges for CI operations

title: Build approval shell UI (stable container component)
description:
Implement the stable, consistent approval container UI that wraps all approval types. This is the "approval shell" — the consistent frame that every approval renders within, regardless of payload kind.

The shell must always answer at a glance:
- What is being approved? (type + short title + system of record)
- What will happen if approved? (high-level effects summary)
- Who is asking and why? (requestor/agent + objective)
- How risky is it? (risk score + why + blast radius indicator)
- What policy gates apply? (SoD/N-of-M/specialist requirements)
- What is the deadline? (SLA / operational urgency)
- What evidence exists? (attachments, diffs, previews, prior runs)

UX requirements:
- Card-based layout suitable for both list view and detail view
- Consistent action bar: Approve / Request Changes / Deny (with rationale input for negative outcomes)
- Prior decisions visible (who else has reviewed, what they decided)
- Escalation action: "needs review by X" handoff
- Responsive: works in cockpit sidebar, full-page, and embedded contexts
- Shell renders the selected domain renderer (from renderer registry) in its content area

title: Research Integration: Cockpit UI/UX & HCI (report-24)
description:
Epic to systematically integrate findings from docs/research/report-24.md — a deep HCI and code quality review of the Cockpit UI against Nielsen heuristics, WCAG, ISO 9241, and engineering best practices.

**Report summary**: Cockpit has a solid card-based foundation with keyboard-first interactions and clear content hierarchy, but suffers from critical UX and code quality gaps: missing action feedback (no spinners or confirmation dialogs), inconsistent navigation and visual patterns, inadequate accessibility (no skip-link, missing ARIA labels, colour-reliant status badges), a 750+ line TriageCard component, hardcoded demo data in production UI, and no error boundaries or loading states.

**Scope**: Full read → validate → implement cycle covering all sections: UX design artefacts, HCI theory alignment, user flows, accessibility, error handling, performance/scalability, maintainability, and the prioritised remediation roadmap.

**Key themes from the report**:
- Interaction feedback: no loading spinners, no confirmation modals for destructive actions, no optimistic UI updates — violates Nielsen heuristic #1 (visibility of system status)
- Navigation consistency: inconsistent breadcrumb usage, some pages orphaned from main nav, back-button behaviour unreliable
- Accessibility: no skip-navigation link, missing aria-label on icon buttons, status badges convey meaning purely through colour (WCAG 1.4.1 failure), no keyboard trap management in modals
- Component architecture: TriageCard is 750+ lines — needs decomposition into focused sub-components with clear prop contracts
- Hardcoded demo data: production UI contains demo/seed data paths that should never ship (security and trust risk)
- Error handling: no React error boundaries, no empty-state components, async errors silently fail
- Performance: no virtualisation on long lists, no code-splitting on heavy routes

**Exit criteria**: All child beads closed or explicitly deferred; Cockpit passes a basic WCAG AA automated audit (axe-core); TriageCard is decomposed; no hardcoded demo data paths remain.

title: RAG tenancy isolation model — workspace-scoped vector + graph retrieval
description: Vector and graph backends for RAG need explicit tenancy and auth models matching workspace boundaries. Decide: vector DB shards-per-workspace (stronger isolation) vs filters-per-workspace (simpler, leakage risk). Graph DB: multi-database per workspace vs shared graph with row-level security. Define authorisation unit for retrieval. Enforce isolation in storage layer, not only HTTP. Test: cross-workspace query must never return another workspace data.

title: OpenSSF Best Practices badge + SLSA provenance mapping
description:
Pursue the OpenSSF Best Practices Badge as a signal that Portarium follows established security and quality practices. The badge programme provides criteria and self-certification.

Prerequisites (must be done first):
- LICENSE file exists
- SECURITY.md exists
- Contribution norms documented

SLSA mapping work:
- Define build provenance generation (where, when, how artefacts are produced)
- Implement provenance attestations for release artefacts
- Document verification steps for consumers
- Map current CI/CD to SLSA levels and identify gaps

Note: this bead is blocked by LICENSE and SECURITY.md — without those, badge work is disconnected from real project readiness.

title: CI-to-cloud OIDC trust policy hardening
description: OIDC federation for CI (GitHub Actions to cloud). Mis-scoped trust policies and missing claim constraints cause pipeline fragility or over-privilege. Deliverables: trust policy review with claim constraints, least privilege CI identity, trust boundary documentation, rejection tests for wrong branch/repo/org, OIDC token exchange audit trail.

title: Research Integration: Security (report-29)
description:
Epic to systematically integrate findings from docs/research/report-29.md — a security audit of Portarium's identity and access management, secrets and cryptography, network and deployment security, and development/QA security practices. Rated P0.

**Report summary**: The control plane uses JWTs (JWKS URI) with a dev-mode static bearer token fallback, workspace-scoped RBAC with optional OpenFGA integration, HashiCorp Vault for credential secrets, and in-memory per-workspace rate limiting. Parameterised SQL queries and structured error responses are present. However, critical gaps exist across all four security domains.

**Scope**: Full read → validate → implement cycle. Security findings must be triaged against current state before acting — some may have been addressed. Any finding that exposes credentials, authentication bypass, or privilege escalation is P0 and must be addressed before the next release.

**Key themes from the report**:

### Identity and Access Management
- Dev-mode static bearer token: the fallback static token path must not be reachable in any non-local environment; needs env-gate or removal
- JWT validation gaps: check that 'alg: none' is rejected, that audience and issuer claims are validated, that token expiry is enforced
- OpenFGA integration: optional but inconsistently applied — workspaces without OpenFGA may have weaker access control than intended; needs audit
- Role escalation surface: no tests verifying that workspace-role boundaries cannot be crossed via API

### Secrets and Cryptography
- Vault credential store: verify that credentials are rotated, that lease expiry is handled gracefully, and that fallback-on-Vault-unavailable does not expose secrets
- SHA-256 evidence hashing: is the pre-image attack surface relevant? Is the hash chain verifiable end-to-end?
- Random generation: verify cryptographically random IDs are used everywhere an ID is security-relevant (no Math.random())
- Environment secrets: check .env handling — secrets must never be logged, must not appear in error messages or stack traces

### Network and Deployment
- HTTPS enforcement: verify TLS termination is enforced in all environments, not just local dev
- CORS configuration: review allowed origins, ensure wildcard CORS is not present in non-dev configs
- Rate limiting: in-memory rate limiter won't survive pod restarts or multi-replica deployment — needs Redis-backed distributed store
- Secrets in container images: verify no secrets baked into Dockerfile layers or K8s manifests

### Development and QA
- Security scanning: verify Snyk/npm audit covers all workspaces including apps/cockpit
- No pen-test or OWASP ZAP scan evidence — add to CI
- Security test cases: no automated tests verifying auth rejection (401/403 on missing/invalid tokens)

**Exit criteria**: All P0/P1 findings fixed and verified; auth rejection test suite added to CI; HTTPS enforcement documented; rate-limit distributed store in place or deferred with documented risk acceptance.

title: Implement evidence-first audit trail for approvals
description:
Build the evidence pipeline that ensures every approval interaction produces durable, auditable records. Every run must produce evidence you can audit — this is a core Portarium promise.

Evidence events to capture:
- approval_opened: who requested, what payload (hash), what policy evaluated to
- policy_evaluated: policy inputs, results, explanations, timestamp
- preview_generated: what was rendered for the approver, inputs used
- assigned: who was assigned, why (policy-driven or manual)
- decision_recorded: who decided, what they decided, rationale, what they saw (payload hash), timestamp
- run_unblocked: approval triggered downstream execution
- effects_applied: what actually happened post-approval
- rollback_executed: if rollback was needed, what was reverted

Requirements:
- Evidence objects are immutable, append-only
- Content-addressable: evidence references payload by hash so tampering is detectable
- Queryable: "show me everything that happened for approval X" timeline view
- Exportable: compliance teams can export evidence for external audit
- Linkage: evidence links to the exact approval payload snapshot, not mutable state
- Special handling for agent/robot requests: capture exactly what the human saw and decided

This is critical for regulated environments and for answering: "what did the human actually see and decide?"

title: Implement block-based presentation DSL for approval evidence
description:
Design and implement the typed block system that agents/workflows use to compose approval evidence. This is the "Notion-style blocks for approval evidence" concept — a strict schema-driven presentation layer that prevents arbitrary UI injection while enabling rich, domain-specific views.

Block types to implement:
- SummaryBlock: human-readable intent + objective + requestor
- PolicyBlock: SoD requirement, required approvers, policy explanations
- EffectsBlock: structured list of planned external effects (resources, operations, blast radius)
- DiffBlock: domain-specific diff rendering (table diff, JSON Patch, text tracked-changes)
- PreviewBlock: renderable output (image, PDF snapshot, MP4 demo, rendered HTML)
- SimulationBlock: dry-run output, predicted outcomes, rollback plan
- AIExplainBlock: model summary, confidence, key reasons + "show sources" links

Design constraints:
- Each block has a strict TypeScript schema; agents can only supply content matching the schema
- Blocks reference evidence payloads by hash/ID (content-addressable)
- Agents choose which blocks to include and in what order, but only from allow-listed set
- Agents can propose content but never execution (no arbitrary HTML/JS)
- Rendering is deterministic from block data — same data always produces same view
- JSON Patch (RFC 6902) as universal baseline for "what changed" representation

title: Implement security controls for agent-supplied approval content
description:
Implement the security boundary that prevents agents from injecting arbitrary UI/code through approval payloads while still allowing rich content presentation.

Threat model:
- Agents supply untrusted strings, rich text, URLs, images, videos
- Malicious or buggy agents could attempt XSS, data exfiltration, privilege escalation
- Agent-authored "layouts" could create misleading presentations that trick approvers

Security controls:
- Default to framework escaping + strict encoding/sanitisation for all agent-supplied content
- Restrict escape hatches: no direct DOM injection, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML for agent content
- Rich text sanitisation: allowlisted HTML tags/attributes only (no script, no event handlers)
- Strict Content Security Policy (CSP): reduce blast radius of any injection — no inline scripts, restricted connect-src
- URL validation: agent-supplied URLs must be allowlisted or proxied
- Image/media: proxy through Portarium CDN, no direct external loads (prevents tracking pixels, SSRF)
- Block schema validation: agent-supplied blocks must pass strict TypeScript schema validation before rendering
- Agents can propose content but never execution

Testing:
- XSS test suite against all block renderers with adversarial inputs
- CSP violation monitoring and alerting
- Fuzzing agent-supplied block content against schema validators

title: Architecture: replace in-memory stub defaults with fail-fast guards in bootstrap
description: From report-31 bead-8lup. Blocked by bead-lh3q (validate task). The control plane bootstrap uses in-memory or empty stub stores by default, meaning a deployment that omits required store configuration ships a silently non-functional system. Every store registration that defaults to an in-memory stub must instead throw a clear startup error unless explicitly configured for dev/test mode via an environment variable. Scope: (1) Audit the composition root / bootstrap file; list every store with a default in-memory implementation. (2) Add a startupGuard() helper that checks required env vars and throws a structured ConfigurationError with a helpful message if any required store is not configured. (3) Add a DEV_STUB_STORES=true env var that must be explicitly set to allow stubs (default off in production). (4) Add a startup integration test that confirms the process exits non-zero if required stores are not configured. Acceptance criteria: app fails to start with clear error if required stores are missing config; DEV_STUB_STORES gate is documented in .env.local.example; startup test in CI.
close_reason: Added store-bootstrap-gate.ts with checkStoreBootstrapGate(). Bootstrap throws FATAL if no Postgres config and DEV_STUB_STORES≠true. DEV_STUB_STORES=true allowed only in dev/test NODE_ENV. 11 unit tests. README documents the requirement. control-plane.test.ts updated to set DEV_STUB_STORES=true.

title: Testing: unit tests for pack resolver and SOD evaluator
description: From report-27 bead-cyut. Blocked by bead-o0d3. Pack resolver and SOD evaluator have zero test coverage — these are core business logic. See validate task for confirmed scope.
close_reason: Already resolved: pack-resolver.test.ts (7 tests) and sod-constraints-v1.test.ts (25 tests) + sod-eligibility-v1.test.ts (14 tests) exist. See report-27.md triage.

title: Research Integration: Core CS Foundations (report-25)
description:
Epic to systematically integrate findings from docs/research/report-25.md — a review of core computer science foundations as applied in Portarium: programming fundamentals, OOP quality, data structures, algorithms, OS fundamentals (concurrency, I/O), and computing ethics context.

**Report summary**: Portarium is structured as a layered hexagonal/ports-and-adapters system with a clean split between control-plane (HTTP API + governance boundary) and execution-plane (worker behaviour, optional Temporal loop). The report evaluates how well foundational CS principles are applied: SOLID adherence, abstraction quality, appropriate data structure choices, algorithmic complexity, concurrency model soundness, and ethical computing considerations.

**Scope**: Full read → validate → implement cycle. The CS foundations review is likely to surface refactoring candidates and algorithmic improvements rather than entirely new features.

**Key themes from the report**:
- OOP & SOLID: identify classes/modules violating Single Responsibility, Open/Closed, or Dependency Inversion; map against the current layer structure
- Data structures: verify correct structure choices in hot paths (pack resolver, SOD evaluation, rate-limiter — maps vs arrays vs sets)
- Algorithms: review time complexity of key domain operations; flag any O(n²) patterns on lists that could grow large
- Concurrency: review async/await usage, identify any unhandled promise rejection risks, check for race conditions in multi-step workflows
- OS fundamentals: I/O patterns (buffered vs streaming), file descriptor management, signal handling in the worker process
- Ethics context: fairness considerations in approval workflows, audit trail completeness, bias surface in policy evaluation

**Exit criteria**: All child beads closed or deferred; a docs/adr/ entry covers any significant algorithmic or concurrency architecture decisions made as a result.

title: Local schema bootstrap — create tables/indexes + deterministic reset command
description: Local Postgres is running is not enough; you need guaranteed schema and deterministic reset. Deliverables: migration runner that applies all schema (tables, indexes, constraints) on first boot, reset command that drops and recreates (for dev iteration), idempotent apply (safe to re-run), integration with dev:seed workflow. Must work both for bare-metal Postgres and Compose-managed Postgres.
close_reason: Implemented: scripts/db/init-local.mjs + reset-local.mjs, dev:db:init + dev:db:reset npm scripts, .env.local.example. All acceptance criteria met.

title: Local auth story — dev-only token mode or bundled OIDC/JWKS provider
description: Protected routes return 401 when auth config is absent, structurally preventing most API usage and Cockpit connection. Cockpit accepts bearer token via VITE_PORTARIUM_API_BEARER_TOKEN or localStorage. Options: (a) bundled Keycloak/Dex in Compose auth profile with pre-seeded users, or (b) dev-only token mode that bypasses JWKS validation in local dev. Either way, the local-first workflow must include how to mint or provide a token deterministically. This is a functional blocker for all real local API usage.
close_reason: DevTokenAuthentication adapter implemented with PORTARIUM_DEV_TOKEN env var; 13 tests, full production guard. Merged to main.

title: Local-First Developer Experience — one-command seeded stack
description:
Tracks the missing DX work to achieve a robust local-first developer experience. Currently Portarium supports a multi-step local run (Compose + separate runtime commands), but lacks:

- Single-command boot to "system usable" state
- Predictable persistence (schema present, data survives restarts, reset is explicit)
- Predictable auth (local OIDC or dev-only auth bypass)
- Smokeable E2E scenario (governed run producing evidence)

Existing bead-0733 covers the seed command itself; this epic covers the surrounding infrastructure (Compose profiles, auth, schema, DevContainer, smoke tests) that make bead-0733 actually work end-to-end.

Target: `npm run dev:all` → everything running, seeded, Cockpit connectable, one governed run demonstrable.

title: Domain: approval content sanitization — untrusted payload encoding and constraint enforcement
Domain: extended presentation blocks — diff, comparison, preview, and AI explanation block types
Domain: approval snapshot binding — immutable content hash anchoring for approved payloads
Domain: approval decision record — structured capture of decisions with justification and policy context
Infra: add bounded body parsing to control-plane HTTP handler (max bytes + abort + 413)
Docs: privacy and ethics purpose-limitation documentation
Domain: approval policy rules
Domain: approval delegation model
Domain: approval escalation engine
Domain: approval context assembler
Cockpit unit tests: core UI components (Vitest + RTL, ≥80% coverage)
body: Write Vitest + React Testing Library unit tests for all core Cockpit UI components: RunStatusChip, EffectsList, EvidenceTimeline, ApprovalDecisionForm, ErrorBanner, ApprovalGatePanel, HumanTaskDrawer, ProvenanceJourney, WorkflowBuilder nodes. Target ≥80% line/branch coverage on apps/cockpit/src/components/cockpit/. Each component tested in all states (pending/success/failed/error). Integrate coverage gate into npm run ci:pr. Ref ADR-0062.

title: Storybook stories for all Cockpit UI components
body: Create @storybook/react-vite stories for every domain component in apps/cockpit/src/components/cockpit/. Each story covers all visual states (normal, loading, error, empty, disabled). Add @storybook/addon-a11y for accessibility checks. Stories serve as living documentation and source for Chromatic visual tests. Path: apps/cockpit/src/components/cockpit/**/*.stories.tsx.

title: Playwright setup and first smoke E2E (approval flow)
body: Set up Playwright (@playwright/test) in the repo. Configure playwright.config.ts with multi-browser projects (Chrome, Firefox, WebKit), retries on CI, and HTML reporter. Write first smoke E2E test: approval flow (login via Keycloak → navigate to Inbox → open Work Item → review → approve with rationale → verify status changes to Executing). Run against test-double/mock API initially. Add npm run test:e2e:smoke script.

title: CI pipeline: integrate Vitest, Storybook, and Playwright into ci:pr gate
body: Extend npm run ci:pr to include: (1) npm run test:unit (Vitest with coverage threshold ≥80%), (2) Storybook build validation (npm run build-storybook), (3) Playwright smoke tests (npm run test:e2e:smoke). All three must pass for PR gate to succeed. Add coverage threshold enforcement via vitest.config coverage.thresholds. Ensure CI runner installs Playwright browsers.

title: Complete Playwright E2E suite: cancel-run, evidence fallback, staleness flows
body: Implement remaining ADR-0062 E2E flows in Playwright: (1) Cancel-run flow — open active run, click Cancel, confirm dialog, verify status=Cancelled and Retry button. (2) Evidence fallback — verify EvidenceTimeline renders chain-of-trust indicators, WORM-lock, hash-mismatch visuals. (3) Staleness/realtime — simulate disconnecting event stream, verify staleness banner appears within ~35s, reconnect and verify banner clears. Add to npm run test:e2e:smoke. Ref ADR-0062 §smoke-flows.

title: Chromatic visual regression on PRs (0.1% diff threshold)
body: Integrate Chromatic into CI. On PRs: run npx chromatic --project-token=... --exit-zero-on-changes. Set diffThreshold: 0.1 in Storybook parameters (global default). On main branch merges: --auto-accept-changes to update baselines. Add CHROMATIC_PROJECT_TOKEN to CI secrets. Chromatic step runs after Storybook build in PR gate. Ref ADR-0062 §visual.

title: Docker Compose local integration stack (Keycloak, OpenFGA, Odoo)
body: Create/finalize docker-compose.local.yml with: Keycloak (OIDC, realm portarium, seeded users alice/bob/carol), OpenFGA (policy engine, seeded workspace roles), Odoo 17 (account+project modules, port 4000→8069), PostgreSQL, Portarium API. Add npm run dev:all script. Add npm run dev:seed:odoo for module installation. Health checks on all services. Document in README. Ref local-integration-guide.

title: Integration tests for Finance/Odoo adapter (recorded fixtures)
body: Write integration tests for the FinanceAccounting adapter against Odoo. Level 1: recorded fixtures (mock Odoo XML-RPC responses). Level 2: round-trip demo against local Odoo container. Test search_read for GL, AR/AP, invoice flows. Use vitest with setup/teardown for Docker container lifecycle. Add to npm run test:integration script.

title: Manual QA checklists and exploratory test scripts
body: Create structured manual QA checklists under docs/qa/: (1) UI smoke checklist (all routes load, sidebar/header visible, no console errors), (2) Approval flow checklist (approve, deny, request-changes with rationale validation), (3) Run management checklist (start, cancel, retry), (4) RBAC checklist (Alice=approver, Bob=operator, Carol=auditor see correct menus/data), (5) Responsive/mobile checklist (<640px viewport, bottom nav, filter-bar wrap), (6) Error handling checklist (RFC 7807 Problem Details, validation alerts). Include pass/fail columns.

title: QA metrics dashboard: coverage, flakiness, pass rate tracking
body: Set up QA metrics collection and reporting: (1) Vitest coverage reports published to Codecov (or similar) on CI, with trend tracking. (2) Playwright flake rate metric: (retried-passes / total-runs), target <1%. (3) CI pass/fail rate tracking. (4) Test duration monitoring (flag tests >30s). (5) Add coverage and build-status badges to README. (6) Define unique-failure tracking to reduce alert fatigue. Review metrics in sprint retros.

title: Domain and infrastructure unit test coverage expansion
body: Extend unit test coverage beyond Cockpit components into domain and infrastructure layers: policy engine rules, pack resolver, SOD logic, approval escalation, evidence chain invariants, workflow invariants. Refactor for testability where needed (introduce interfaces/DI). Target: all src/domain/ modules have ≥70% coverage. Add coverage gates per layer.

title: Full regression E2E suite with RunEmulator
body: Build a comprehensive E2E regression suite (npm run test:e2e:full) for nightly CI runs. Use RunEmulator to simulate complete workflow runs end-to-end. Cover: multi-step approval chains, concurrent runs, permission boundaries (SoD enforcement), evidence audit trail completeness, workflow builder create/edit/validate cycles. Run against full Docker Compose stack.

title: Playwright cross-browser and responsive viewport matrix
body: Expand Playwright config with full test matrix: Desktop Chrome/Firefox/WebKit at 1920x1080, mobile viewports (375x667 Pixel 5, 414x896 iPhone 13). Run responsive UI tests (navigation collapse, filter-bar wrap, bottom nav) across all viewports. Add to nightly CI as matrix strategy. Document supported browsers/viewports.

title: Test data seeding scripts for deterministic E2E runs
body: Create scripts under scripts/qa/ for deterministic test data setup: (1) Seed Keycloak realm with test users and roles. (2) Seed OpenFGA with workspace policies. (3) Seed Odoo with demo accounts/invoices. (4) Seed Portarium API with sample workflows, runs, and approval requests. Scripts must be idempotent (re-runnable). Add npm run test:seed command. Reset state between CI runs via Docker volume recreation.

title: Flaky test detection, quarantine, and hardening
body: Implement flaky test management: (1) Enable Playwright trace-on-first-retry and video-on-failure for diagnosis. (2) Track flake rate per test file over 30-day window. (3) Auto-quarantine tests with >5% flake rate (move to separate suite, don't block CI). (4) Weekly review of quarantined tests — fix root cause or remove. (5) Target: overall flake rate <1%. (6) Add flake-rate badge to CI dashboard.

title: Campaign: enforce agent outbound routing through Portarium control plane
body:
Deliver a defense-in-depth implementation that ensures AI machine runtimes and related components cannot call external systems directly.

Scope:
- Use Portarium Agent Gateway and Sidecar Proxy as mandatory control points.
- Enforce default-deny outbound posture with no direct egress bypass.
- Ensure identity, policy, audit, and rollout controls are production ready.

Completion criteria:
- All linked implementation, security, observability, testing, release, and local-scenario validation beads are closed.
- CI evidence demonstrates blocked bypass attempts and successful policy-governed egress.

title: ADR: mandatory sidecar-or-gateway enforcement model for agent egress
body:
Write an ADR that formalizes the enforcement architecture for outbound agent calls.

Acceptance criteria:
- Defines Pattern A (Action API interception) and Pattern B (Sidecar/Gateway proxy) as baseline.
- Defines fail-closed behavior and no-direct-egress invariant.
- Specifies identity model (JWT + mTLS/SPIFFE), audit requirements, and threat assumptions.
- Includes migration phases and rollback strategy.

title: Application: route OpenClaw tool execution through Portarium Action API
body:
Implement tool-call interception so external operations are represented as Portarium actions.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tool execution path uses action propose/execute flow instead of direct vendor calls.
- Workspace and subject identity are preserved end-to-end.
- Denied actions return clear policy errors.
- Integration tests cover allowed and denied tool calls.

title: Infrastructure: Portarium Sidecar Proxy default-deny egress with transparent interception
body:
Implement and harden sidecar-based egress enforcement for agent workloads.

Acceptance criteria:
- All outbound HTTP/HTTPS is redirected through Sidecar Proxy.
- Egress allowlist is enforced with default-deny behavior.
- Sidecar injects required identity and trace context headers.
- Denials are logged with destination and policy reason.

title: Security: enforce no-bypass egress with NetworkPolicy and mesh egress gateway controls
body:
Add network-layer controls that prevent bypassing sidecar or gateway enforcement.

Acceptance criteria:
- Cluster policy denies direct outbound from agent pods except approved proxy paths.
- Mesh egress gateway routing is configured for approved destinations.
- Control is tested against sidecar-disabled and direct-IP bypass attempts.
- Policy-as-code manifests are versioned and validated in CI.

title: Security: unify agent and proxy identity via workspace JWT and SPIFFE mTLS
body:
Harden identity guarantees for all egress control points.

Acceptance criteria:
- Agent Gateway validates workspace JWT claims and rejects mismatched scope.
- Sidecar uses SPIFFE/SPIRE mTLS identity and rotates credentials safely.
- Identity propagation fields are standardized for audit correlation.
- Dev-mode token paths are explicitly gated from production.

title: Infrastructure: fail-closed proxy behavior and resilient restart strategy
body:
Ensure outages do not create accidental direct egress paths.

Acceptance criteria:
- Proxy unavailability blocks outbound requests by default.
- Health checks and restart policies recover failed sidecars/gateways.
- Error semantics are explicit (for example 502/503 with correlation ID).
- Chaos/failure tests prove no bypass during degraded states.

title: Cross-cutting: egress observability and evidence chain for gateway and sidecar
body:
Complete logging, metrics, and traceability for governed egress.

Acceptance criteria:
- Structured logs include workspace, run, subject, and correlation identifiers.
- Trace context is propagated across agent, gateway, sidecar, and connector hops.
- Metrics cover allow/deny counts, latency, errors, and token/cert expiry.
- Evidence entries can link external calls to the originating action/run.

title: Integration: prove outbound-governance invariants with bypass and policy tests
body:
Build integration tests that verify only policy-governed outbound access is possible.

Acceptance criteria:
- Allowed destination calls succeed via gateway/sidecar path.
- Direct egress attempts are blocked and auditable.
- Threat scenarios include metadata endpoint access attempts and raw socket-style bypass.
- CI gate fails when bypass or missing audit fields are detected.

title: Integration: benchmark gateway and sidecar latency/throughput envelopes
body:
Define and validate performance budgets for enforced routing.

Acceptance criteria:
- Measure baseline versus enforced path and report p50/p95 latency and throughput at fixed load levels.
- Pass/fail thresholds:
  - p95 added latency <= 25 ms for governed dispatch path at reference load.
  - Throughput degradation <= 20 percent versus baseline.
  - Error rate <= 1 percent under steady-state load.
- Validate rate limits, retries, and timeout behavior under stress.
- If thresholds fail, create follow-up mitigation beads with owners before closure.

title: Release: phased migration and CI/CD guardrails for enforced outbound routing
body:
Operationalize rollout from pilot to full enforcement.

Acceptance criteria:
- Deployment runbook covers sidecar injection, proxy config, and policy tightening phases.
- CI/CD validates egress policy config drift and environment wiring.
- Rollout includes staged adoption plan and rollback steps.
- Production readiness checklist includes security, observability, and incident response hooks.

title: Campaign: local control-plane test scenarios for runs, approvals, and SoR governance
body:
Implement a delta end-to-end local scenario suite proving Portarium control-plane routing and approval enforcement.

Scope:
- Extend existing local stack and seed capabilities (do not recreate prior closed work).
- Cover OpenClaw machine dispatch, Odoo finance adapter flow, approval-gated runs, auth/authz negative paths, policy bypass prevention, and seed-data validation.
- Produce reproducible verification artifacts for local and CI.

Non-goals:
- Rebuilding docker compose or seed foundations already delivered in earlier beads.

Completion criteria:
- Scenario tests are runnable locally and in CI.
- Evidence/log/metrics checks prove all external-effecting operations traverse Portarium endpoints.
- Traceability notes map each scenario to prior baseline beads and the new delta assertions.

title: Integration harness delta: deterministic scenario bootstrap and dual-auth profile checks
body:
Create a deterministic delta harness for local control-plane scenario execution.

Context:
- Extend existing local stack/seed assets; do not duplicate compose or seed foundations from previously closed beads.

Acceptance criteria:
- One command runs required services and verifies health with a machine-readable readiness report.
- Harness supports both auth profiles used by tests: dev-token mode and OIDC/JWKS mode.
- Seed orchestration supports baseline seed and Odoo-specific reseed idempotently.
- Troubleshooting guidance is included for startup/auth/seed failures.

title: Scenario delta: OpenClaw machine dispatch via /runs with evidence and trace verification
body:
Automate the OpenClaw machine dispatch scenario through Portarium run APIs as a regression-strengthening delta.

Acceptance criteria:
- Creates a run through workspace /runs endpoint with a simple machine action.
- Verifies run transitions to Succeeded with stable polling/backoff rules.
- Verifies evidence contains dispatch/completion entries and correlation identifiers.
- Captures API and worker log markers proving control-plane dispatch path.
- Documents what is new beyond prior governed-run smoke coverage.

title: Scenario delta: Odoo FinanceAccounting run-path verification with seeded-data assertions
body:
Automate finance adapter scenario against local seeded Odoo as a run-path/evidence delta over adapter-only tests.

Acceptance criteria:
- Executes FinanceAccounting operation via Portarium run step (no direct Odoo call from scenario actor).
- Verifies seeded business data expectations through run outputs/evidence.
- Verifies evidence includes adapter action metadata, result summary, and correlation context.
- Includes diagnostics for Odoo startup/seed timing and credential issues.
- Documents what additional guarantees this scenario adds beyond existing Odoo adapter tests.

title: Scenario delta: HumanApprove pause/resume with maker-checker and decision audit chain
body:
Automate approval-gate scenario requiring manual decision, focusing on deterministic policy/audit assertions.

Acceptance criteria:
- Starts run in HumanApprove tier and verifies paused/pending state.
- Resolves approval through approvals API using authorized approver identity.
- Validates maker-checker constraints with negative assertion for ineligible approver.
- Verifies resumed run completes and evidence records approval requested/resolved/completed chain.
- Documents incremental coverage versus prior approval-gated E2E tests.

title: Security scenario: auth/authz negative-path contract checks (401/403) for control-plane endpoints
body:
Implement negative-path tests for authentication and authorization enforcement.

Acceptance criteria:
- Missing token and invalid token requests return 401 with contract-compliant envelope.
- Insufficient role/scope requests return 403.
- Assertions are executed in both auth profiles: dev-token mode and OIDC/JWKS mode.
- Assertions include response body contract plus audit-log markers.
- Tests cover key endpoints used by dispatch and approvals flows.

title: Security scenario: policy enforcement check for control-plane bypass attempts (409)
body:
Validate rejection behavior for attempts to bypass governed execution flow.

Acceptance criteria:
- Test A: submit a run/action payload that targets a non-allowlisted external effect; API returns contract-compliant 409 policy error.
- Test B: attempt direct external-effect call path from scenario actor without approved run/action route; request is denied and audit marker is emitted.
- For both tests, assert HTTP status, policy error code, and evidence/log entry containing denial reason and correlation ID.
- Includes regression test that fails if bypass path is accidentally permitted.

title: Cross-cutting: scenario observability pack (logs, metrics, evidence chain, Temporal history)
body:
Add explicit verification artifacts for scenario runs.

Acceptance criteria:
- Scenario runner emits links/paths to relevant API, worker, and integration logs.
- Metrics checks include error counts and success ratios for scenario endpoints.
- Evidence chain validation confirms expected event sequence and integrity markers.
- Temporal workflow history checks are captured for dispatch and approval scenarios.

title: Docs: local scenario matrix, exact commands, expected outputs, and troubleshooting
body:
Publish an operator-friendly local test plan aligned to implemented scenarios.

Acceptance criteria:
- Includes per-scenario prerequisites, seed steps, command sequence, and verification checks.
- Includes comparison matrix (components, setup time, runtime, key assertions).
- Includes troubleshooting section for token, OpenFGA, Odoo, and worker failures.
- Uses glossary-consistent terms and references applicable ADR/control-plane contracts.

title: CI gate delta: control-plane scenario suite in ci:pr with deterministic fixtures and artifacts
body:
Operationalize scenario coverage as an additive CI quality gate.

Context:
- Extend existing ci:pr gates; do not replace or duplicate prior test-stage wiring.

Acceptance criteria:
- Adds CI target for scenario suite with deterministic seed and teardown.
- Fails build on missing evidence sequence, auth contract drift, or bypass-policy regressions.
- Publishes scenario artifacts (logs, evidence snapshots, summaries) to CI outputs.
- Documents runtime budget and split strategy for PR vs nightly execution.

title: Quality gate fix: resolve ci:gates baseline drift for package.json with required ADR linkage
body:
`npm run ci:pr` fails at gate baseline due package.json hash mismatch against .ci/gate-baseline.json.

Acceptance criteria:
- Root-cause is identified (expected package change vs stale baseline process defect).
- If gate file intent changed, baseline is regenerated and committed with ADR documenting rationale.
- If package drift is unintended, restore package alignment without baseline drift.
- ci:gates passes on clean checkout.

title: Repo hygiene: prevent tmp/local-scratch artifacts from breaking lint and CI gates
body:
`npm run lint` fails because tmp/local-scratch TypeScript artifacts are picked up by ESLint project service.

Acceptance criteria:
- Transient scratch/tmp paths are excluded from lint/format/test discovery or auto-cleaned before gates.
- CI and local runs are deterministic regardless of local scratch files.
- Repo hygiene docs include expected temp paths and cleanup command.

title: Formatting gate alignment: resolve Prettier check failure (348-file drift) with maintainable policy
body:
`npm run format:check` reports large-scale formatting drift.

Acceptance criteria:
- Preferred path: apply canonical formatting to affected tracked source files.
- Alternate path (exception-only): narrow format-check scope only for generated/transient paths (for example build artifacts, caches, temp folders), never for tracked source paths under src/, apps/, scripts/, docs/, or tests.
- Any exception path requires ADR documentation with rationale, risk, and rollback plan.
- Policy remains consistent across local and CI workflows.
- `npm run format:check` passes on a clean checkout.

title: Cockpit unit-test toolchain repair: restore missing @testing-library/dom dependency path
body:
`npm run test:unit` fails in cockpit workspace with `Cannot find module @testing-library/dom`.

Acceptance criteria:
- Required testing-library dependency graph is explicit in workspace package manifests.
- Lockfile is synchronized and reproducible under npm ci.
- cockpit coverage test command passes in CI environment.

title: Cockpit Storybook gate repair: make build-storybook executable in ci:pr
body:
`npm run cockpit:build-storybook` fails with `storybook is not recognized`.

Acceptance criteria:
- Storybook CLI dependency and scripts are correctly wired for workspace execution on CI/local.
- Storybook build command succeeds non-interactively.
- Any migration (package rename or script changes) is documented.

title: Review gate B: pre-close cross-discipline verification and residual-risk signoff
body:
Run a formal pre-close review pass before campaign closure.

Acceptance criteria:
- Architecture verification confirms implemented behavior matches approved design and boundaries.
- Security verification confirms bypass prevention, identity enforcement, and auditability in executed tests.
- Test/V&V verification confirms deterministic scenario evidence and reproducible diagnostics.
- Residual risks and deferred findings are captured with explicit owners and follow-up beads.

title: Refactor for clarity: shared scenario assertion helpers and fixture contracts
body:
Improve maintainability/readability of new scenario suite.

Acceptance criteria:
- Shared assertion helpers remove duplicated polling/evidence/auth checks across scenarios.
- Fixture contracts are centralized with typed helpers and clear naming.
- Scenario files stay focused on behavior, not plumbing.
- Added tests guard helper behavior.

title: Review gate A: pre-implementation architecture/security/V&V inspection
body:
Run a formal pre-implementation inspection before major execution begins.

Acceptance criteria:
- Architecture review confirms boundaries, contracts, and control-plane choke-point invariants.
- Security review confirms threat assumptions, bypass controls, and identity posture.
- V&V review confirms deterministic testability, evidence assertions, and failure diagnostics design.
- Findings are dispositioned (must-fix-now vs follow-up bead) before implementation closure work proceeds.

title: Cockpit toolchain alignment: unify test + Storybook build dependencies and scripts
body:
Coordinate cockpit build/test toolchain fixes as one cohesive change set.

Acceptance criteria:
- Root-cause matrix covers unit-test dependency resolution and Storybook CLI execution failures.
- Fixes for test and Storybook are compatible in lockfile and workspace scripts.
- Validation runs include cockpit unit coverage and cockpit storybook build in CI-like environment.
- Closure requires child beads for unit-test dependency repair and Storybook gate repair to be completed.

title: Campaign: agent governance end-to-end — propose, approve, execute pipeline
body: Deliver the full agent-action governance pipeline: proposeAgentAction wired to real deps, approval store integration, execution after approval, and contract tests covering the complete flow. Depends on all child beads being closed.

title: Toolchain: upgrade Node.js minimum to v20 LTS
body: ESLint 9 with eslint-plugin-unicorn requires Array.prototype.toReversed() (Node 20+). Vitest coverage uses node:inspector/promises (Node 20+). Update engines field in package.json, CI matrix, Dockerfile base images, and .nvmrc to Node 20 LTS. Verify all ci:pr gates pass on Node 20.

title: CI: update gate baseline after Node 20 upgrade and agent-governance commit
body: Regenerate .ci/gate-baseline.json to account for package.json and config changes from the Node 20 upgrade and the proposeAgentAction commit. Document in ADR per ADR-0041 policy.

title: Application: wire proposeAgentAction handler to real ControlPlaneDeps
body: The control-plane-handler.agent-actions.ts handler currently builds inline stub deps with `as never` casts for policyStore, approvalStore, eventPublisher, evidenceLog, and unitOfWork. Wire these to the real ControlPlaneDeps that are bootstrapped in control-plane-handler.bootstrap.ts. Remove all `as never` casts. Add the new route to the contract test suite.

title: Presentation: add approval CRUD endpoints to control plane (GET/POST /approvals)
body: Add HTTP endpoints for approval lifecycle: GET /v1/workspaces/:id/approvals (list), GET /v1/workspaces/:id/approvals/:approvalId (poll), POST /v1/workspaces/:id/approvals/:approvalId/decide (approve/deny). Wire to the existing ApprovalStore port. These endpoints are referenced in the demo proxy but not yet in the real control plane (currently returning 404 from the catch-all).

title: Application: approval decision command with SoD maker-checker enforcement
body: Implement the DecideApproval use case: validate that the deciding user is not the same as the requesting user (maker-checker), check SoD constraints from policy, record the decision with evidence, emit ApprovalGranted/ApprovalDenied domain events. Tests must cover: happy path approve, happy path deny, maker-checker violation, SoD violation, approval not found, already decided.

title: Application: executeApprovedAgentAction command — post-approval tool dispatch
body: After an agent action proposal is approved, the agent needs to execute the tool. Implement executeApprovedAgentAction that: verifies the approval exists and is granted, verifies the caller matches the original proposal agent, dispatches through the action-gated tool invoker, records execution evidence, emits ActionDispatched/ActionCompleted/ActionFailed events.

title: Integration: agent-action proposal and approval contract test suite
body: End-to-end contract tests covering the full lifecycle: POST propose → poll approval → POST decide → execute. Cover: Allow flow (ReadOnly tool, Auto tier), NeedsApproval flow (Mutation tool, HumanApprove tier with maker-checker), Denied flow (Dangerous tool), workspace mismatch rejection, auth/authz negative paths (401/403). Tests must run in ci:pr.

title: Fix: validate run status query parameter in control-plane-handler
body: In control-plane-handler.ts line ~312, rawStatus is cast with `as never` instead of being validated/narrowed to the RunStatus branded type. Add proper validation that rejects invalid status values with a 422 and test the validation.

title: Integration: wire ADR-0117 companion CLI approval-wait loop to control plane
body: The approval-wait loop experiments (ADR-0117) determined H7 companion CLI as the winner. The demo implementation exists in scripts/demo/ but is not wired to the real control plane approval endpoints. Integrate the companion CLI mechanism with the real POST /approvals/:id/decide endpoint and the proposeAgentAction flow. Update the demo proxy to use real endpoints when available.

title: Spec: agent action governance lifecycle specification
body: Write .specify/specs/agent-action-governance-lifecycle-v1.md covering: ProposeAgentAction input/output contract, policy evaluation rules, tool blast-radius classification matrix, approval routing rules, execution gating, evidence chain requirements, maker-checker SoD invariants.

title: ADR: agent action governance architecture (propose/approve/execute)
body: Write docs/internal/adr/ADR-0118-agent-action-governance.md documenting the architecture decisions for the agent action governance pipeline: why propose/approve/execute instead of direct invocation, how tool classification drives approval routing, how SoD maker-checker is enforced, how evidence chain maintains auditability.

title: Fix: resolve gRPC method binding type casts in robotics-gateway
body: grpc-mission-gateway.ts has three `as never` casts on gRPC method bindings (lines ~149, 211, 234) indicating type misalignment with generated protobuf client stubs. Investigate whether the generated types need regeneration or the binding pattern needs adjustment. Remove the casts.

title: Domain: AgentActionProposalV1 aggregate — typed domain model for proposals
body: Define AgentActionProposalV1 in src/domain/machines/ with: proposalId (branded), workspaceId, agentId, machineId?, actionKind, toolName, parameters?, executionTier, toolClassification, policyDecision, decision, approvalId?, occurredAtIso, correlationId. Add parser with validation. This enables the AgentActionProposalStore port to use a typed aggregate. Acceptance: parser tests cover all field variants; no external dependencies; dependency-cruiser passes.

title: Domain: add agent action lifecycle event types to DomainEventType catalogue
body: domain-events-v1.ts has AgentActionProposed but is missing downstream lifecycle events. Add: AgentActionApprovalRequested, AgentActionApproved, AgentActionDenied, AgentActionExecuted, AgentActionExecutionFailed. Update the EVENT_TYPES set and parser tests.

title: Application: AgentActionProposalStore port — durable proposal persistence for resume
body: Define AgentActionProposalStore port in src/application/ports/ with getProposalById(tenantId, proposalId) and saveProposal(tenantId, proposal). The proposeAgentAction command writes evidence but no typed proposal record is persisted for later retrieval by executeApprovedAgentAction. In-memory adapter for tests; PostgreSQL adapter for production.

title: Infrastructure: PostgreSQL adapter for AgentActionProposalStore
body: Implement PostgreSQL adapter for AgentActionProposalStore. Store proposals in agent_action_proposals table with tenant-scoped composite key. JSONB for parameters. Expand migration script. ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE for idempotent saves.

title: Application: wire proposeAgentAction NeedsApproval branch to create real ApprovalV1
body: When proposeAgentAction produces decision === NeedsApproval, create and persist a real ApprovalV1 record via deps.approvalStore.saveApproval(). Return the approvalId in ProposeAgentActionOutput (field exists but is never populated). The HTTP handler stub must be replaced with real adapter wiring. Acceptance: NeedsApproval response includes real approvalId; GET /approvals/:id returns the pending approval.

title: Application: idempotent proposeAgentAction — deduplicate on idempotencyKey
body: ProposeAgentActionInput includes idempotencyKey but the command ignores it. Implement deduplication: if idempotencyKey is provided and a proposal with that key exists, return existing proposalId/decision without re-evaluating policy or writing duplicate evidence.

title: Cockpit: approval queue entry for agent action proposals
body: Agent action proposals with NeedsApproval must appear in the Cockpit approvals triage queue. Show: tool name, tool category, blast-radius tier, rationale, agent ID, machine ID. Approving triggers executeApprovedAgentAction.

title: Demo: wire approval plugin to real control-plane API (ADR-0117 H9 path)
body: ADR-0117 defines the H9 migration path: plugin polls real Portarium API instead of demo proxy. Update portarium-approval-plugin.mjs to accept --use-control-plane flag targeting real endpoints. Update companion CLI to call POST /approvals/:id/decide on the real control plane.

title: Infrastructure: wire MachineInvokerPort into executeApprovedAgentAction dispatch
body: The MachineInvokerPort and OpenClawGatewayMachineInvoker exist. Wire into executeApprovedAgentAction command DI so real tool dispatch goes through the gateway. Integration tests use stub implementation.
